description:
You are a technical writer who has decided to keep a tiny SQLite database to track the state of every documentation page in your project.  
Complete the following steps entirely from the Linux terminal ( no GUI tools ).

1. Create a new SQLite database file at  
   /home/user/projects/doc_site/docs.db

2. Inside that database, create **exactly one** table named `doc_pages` with this schema (and in this order):  
   • id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
   • title TEXT  
   • status TEXT

3. Insert **exactly three** rows into `doc_pages`:

   id | title         | status  
   ---|---------------|---------  
   1  | Intro         | published  
   2  | Installation  | draft  
   3  | FAQ           | draft  

4. Produce a plain-text report that lists only the pages whose status is **draft**.  
   • Query your database so that the output is sorted by `id` in ascending order.  
   • Store the query result in the file  
     /home/user/projects/doc_site/reports/draft_pages.log  
   • The file must contain **exactly** the following three lines (three newline characters, Unix line endings):

   id|title|status  
   2|Installation|draft  
   3|FAQ|draft  

   (No leading/trailing spaces, no extra blank lines, and the separator is the **pipe** character “|”.)

The automated checker will verify:

• The database file exists at the precise path given.  
• Table `doc_pages` exists with the exact schema.  
• The three specified rows are present.  
• The report file exists at the path given and matches the three-line, pipe-separated format above **byte-for-byte**.

Tip: you will likely need only three or four terminal commands (e.g., a combination of `mkdir`, `sqlite3`, and shell redirection).  
Do not remove any directories that may already exist; create missing ones as needed.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. File: /home/user/projects/doc_site/docs.db  
   • Contains one table named `doc_pages`.  
   • Schema returned by `pragma table_info(doc_pages);` is:  
     (0,'id','INTEGER',0,null,1)  
     (1,'title','TEXT',0,null,0)  
     (2,'status','TEXT',0,null,0)  
   • `SELECT * FROM doc_pages ORDER BY id;` returns:  
     1|Intro|published  
     2|Installation|draft  
     3|FAQ|draft  

2. File: /home/user/projects/doc_site/reports/draft_pages.log  
   Exact byte content (including final newline):

   id|title|status\n
   2|Installation|draft\n
   3|FAQ|draft\n

(The final newline character after the last line **is** required.)

Directory permissions: any newly created directory should be accessible by the current non-root user (e.g., mode 755).  
No other files are required for verification.
